name: Climate Almanac
description: An open, versioned index of public climate data.

entries.title: Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters
entries.description: Database of US weather/climate disasters each exceeding $1B in damages (CPI-adjusted), 1980-present, with event-level cost, deaths, and type. The canonical record for US climate-disaster cost trends.
entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI), U.S. Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters
entries.notes: THE THESIS ENTRY. NOAA announced in 2025 it would stop updating this database. Historical data through 2024 remains reachable but is no longer maintained — hence status=frozen. This is exactly the kind of small, high-value, at-risk artifact that should be prioritized for an actual mirror (it is a single modest CSV). Verify current reachability before relying on it; if the page 404s, flip status to dark and populate archive.wayback_url + archive.mirror.

entries.title: Climate at a Glance — Global & National Time Series
entries.description: Monthly temperature and precipitation anomalies for the globe, nations, US regions, states, and counties. NCEI's flagship public-facing trend product; the source behind most "warmest year on record" reporting.

access.rate_limit: NCEI access API, no published hard limit; be polite

entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information, Climate at a Glance
entries.notes: Core dataset — high priority to keep mapped. Time series are small (CSV per region) and would be cheap to mirror if it ever darkens.

entries.title: Sea Ice Index
entries.description: Daily and monthly Arctic and Antarctic sea ice extent and concentration, with anomaly fields and trend imagery. The standard reference for sea-ice decline; widely cited and surfaced by climate.gov.
entries.attribution: Fetterer, F. et al. Sea Ice Index, NSIDC, Boulder, Colorado USA
entries.notes: Hosted at NSIDC (a university-based DAAC), so somewhat insulated from federal site shutdowns — but funded partly through NOAA/NASA, so not immune. Worth watching.
